interactive.rationale: Some controls present but limited or no script wiring — interactivity feels under-built.

references.rationale: Page cites chromestatus.com/feature/5083520948977664 but the feature's canonical id is 6352111728852992. This breaks the self-heal anchor (CLAUDE.md rule #3) and misleads readers.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles route through --bg/--text/--accent variables, no raw hex, and button overrides pair background + color correctly.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept's lede mirrors the feature index promise — same nouns, same scope.

spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the keywords the spec/feature-index lede surfaces — covers the headline API surface.

edge_cases.rationale: Page surfaces failure modes (unsupported, rejected, error paths) alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wire the controls to live behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Controls exist but don't appear to drive a live readout. Hook each control to an event listener that re-renders the demo and shows the resulting state.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus id link
openQuestions.detail: Replace the citation of chromestatus.com/feature/5083520948977664 with chromestatus.com/feature/6352111728852992. The wrong id will trip the self-heal script and point readers at an unrelated feature.

openQuestions.title: Add a direct spec link
openQuestions.detail: References list only points back to the feature index or ChromeStatus. Add a direct link to the relevant spec (W3C draft, WICG explainer, or RFC) so readers can reach the normative source in one click.

summary: Mostly solid but with one structural gap (fail on the rubric). The major open question is the priority fix.
